description: baseline: naive single-threaded, Instant.parse, String.split, ArrayList percentiles
indexOf parsing, manual ISO timestamp, StringBuilder output, double[] sliding state
single sort for both p50/p99 percentiles
1MB reader buffer + sensor ID interning via HashMap
quickselect O(n) for percentiles instead of full sort O(n log n)
fast manual double parser (no improvement, within noise)
compact SensorWindow key (no type string), separate emitted sets, fast double parser
array-indexed windows replacing HashMaps entirely
bounded scan with floor/ceiling in emitReadyWindows
emit every 100K instead of 10K (massive 3.2x improvement)
emit only at end, no intermediate emissions
direct sensor index from digits, eliminates HashMap lookup
memory-mapped I/O with byte-level parsing (no improvement vs BufferedReader)
multi-threaded parallel chunk processing with byte[] parsing
independent file handles per thread (no improvement)
simplified sliding window loop (no improvement)
pre-allocate sensor rows + hoist sRow (no improvement, memory waste)
fixed-offset parsing (JIT already optimizes comma scan)
smaller initial SlidingState array (more resizes, no gain)
parallel merge+emit by sensor range
Arrays.parallelSort slower for ~30K results
BufferedWriter output slower than StringBuilder+print
sorted-order emit but lost parallelism in merge+percentiles
Arrays.sort in-place for percentiles (within noise)
JVM flags -Xmx4g -XX:+UseZGC (slower, ZGC overhead)
sorted SlidingState with O(n+m) merge (no improvement over quickselect)
bounded minute ranges in merge (branch predictor already handles null checks)
bucket-sorted emission + in-place percentiles (no improvement, within noise)
per-minute value storage for sliding windows (concatenation overhead negated savings)
flat-indexed output arrays (cache-unfriendly writes to minute-major layout)
zero-alloc percentiles + direct byte output (byte loop slower than StringBuilder)
double[8] sliding arrays + in-place percentiles (10% worse, too many resizes)
bounded merge ranges + combined merge (code overhead exceeds iteration savings)
single-partition processing (eliminate merge): 38% slower, sequential parsing bottleneck
pre-sorted sliding + merge-sort merge: 13% slower, sort+alloc overhead exceeds quickselect savings
sorted-order emit (no Collections.sort), parallel pct computation (3954132 ev/s, +13.6%)
byte[] output buffer with Long.toString().getBytes(): 10% slower, alloc overhead
parallel emit by minute range + BufferedOutputStream (4327131 ev/s, +9.4%)
optimized sliding window loop: int minute iteration, no modulo, no redundant checks (4504504 ev/s, +4.1%)
fixed-offset comma: CORRECTNESS FAIL, sliding window minute calc wrong
fixed-offset comma (corrected): still CORRECTNESS FAIL, sliding window bug
fixed-offset first comma (Z-aware): no improvement, within noise
parallel byte[] conversion in emit threads (4928536 ev/s, +9.4%)
8MB BufferedOutputStream: 5% slower, cache pressure
cached date computation in timestamp: within noise
fast minute from hour:minute + fixed comma offsets: within noise
FileChannel output: no improvement
skip-ahead newline scan + fixed comma offsets: within noise
pipelined emit: no improvement, already overlapping
direct append (no sb2): within noise
pre-computed timestamp prefixes: within noise (5-run confirmation)
nThreads/2 chunks: no improvement, reduced parse parallelism
bundled emit: pre-computed prefixes + direct append, no sb2 (5010020 ev/s, +1.7%)
bounded minute range: negligible savings (~60 empty slots)
fused merge+emit: 5% worse, poor cross-partition cache locality
data layout transposition [sensor][minute] → [minute][sensor] for cache-friendly parse
SlidingState initial array 64→8: resize overhead > memory savings (-5.2%)
SlidingState initial array 64→16: still regresses (-5%), high GC variance
lazy sliding windows: 1 raw write per event instead of 5, combine at merge time (+2.6%)
SoA tumbling (primitive arrays): Arrays.fill init overhead, no improvement
direct minute-index + merged if: within noise, JIT already optimizes
direct byte[] emit: eliminate StringBuilder→String→byte[] triple copy (+8.5%)
digit-pair appendLongBytes: within noise, JIT handles division chains
bypass BufferedOutputStream: regression, batching helps pipe writes
experiment: merged bounds-check in processChunk — single if block for tumbling+sliding (5824111 ev/s, +1.4%)
experiment: hardcoded c1=lineStart+20, scan c2 from c1+8 — skip comma search loop (5885815 ev/s, +1.1%)
experiment: branchless c2 — chained if/else for sensor digit count, no scan loop (5966587 ev/s, +1.4%)
experiment: fused c2+sIdx parse with nested branching — JIT regression (5672149 ev/s, -4.9%)
experiment: single bulk read + parallel parse — 388MB allocation GC pressure (4570383 ev/s, -23.4%)
experiment: bounded merge loops by active minute range per partition (6016847 ev/s, +0.8%)
experiment: single-pass merge — tumbling+sliding fused in one partition/minute loop (6257822 ev/s, +4.0%)
experiment: transpose merged data to [minute][sensor] layout — cache-friendly emit (6447453 ev/s, +3.0%)
experiment: direct tumbling merge to output layout — scattered writes cause cache misses (6226650 ev/s, -3.4%)
experiment: active-range-only allocation and emit — dynamic bounds overhead (6086427 ev/s, -5.6%)
experiment: reuse per-sensor temp arrays with Arrays.fill — fill slower than TLAB alloc (6020469 ev/s, -6.6%)
experiment: Arrays.sort instead of dual quickselect — O(n log n) slower than O(n) (5537098 ev/s, -14.1%)
experiment: two-phase merge (minute-parallel raw + sensor-parallel pcts) — barrier overhead (5959475 ev/s, -7.6%)
experiment: eliminate newline scan, inline double parse — breaks JIT vectorization (6246096 ev/s, -3.1%)
experiment: cache date→minute offset, parse only HH:MM — comparison overhead exceeds savings (5970149 ev/s, -7.4%)
experiment: ForkJoinPool.commonPool() — nCPU-1 threads offset startup savings (6439150 ev/s, ~0%)
experiment: virtual threads — scheduling overhead for CPU-bound work (6188118 ev/s, -4.0%)
experiment: 6 threads — insufficient parallelism (5844535 ev/s, -9.4%)
experiment: 32-bit division for minute index — JIT already uses multiply-by-reciprocal (6119951 ev/s, -5.1%)
experiment: bypass System.out with FileDescriptor.out — pipe buffer contention (5906674 ev/s, -8.4%)
experiment: concat emit chunks before writing — 166MB extra allocation GC pressure (5546311 ev/s, -14.0%)
experiment: merge SlidingState into TumblingState — unified state eliminates redundant array dereferences (6720430 ev/s, +4.2%)
experiment: integer percentile indices + zero-copy emit buffers — eliminate Math.ceil and 168MB trim copies (7283321 ev/s, +8.4%)
experiment: mmap file reading — kernel readahead beats channel.read for parse I/O (7668711 ev/s, +5.3%)
experiment: combined sliding+tumbling emit per task — larger working set hurts cache (7107320 ev/s, -7.3%)
experiment: flat slidingP50/P99 arrays with NaN sentinel — Arrays.fill(NaN) + isNaN overhead outweighs savings (7299270 ev/s, -4.8%)
experiment: single mmap + shared data array — 388MB allocation GC pressure (6756756 ev/s, -11.9%)
experiment: skip 31 bytes before newline scan — known minimum line length avoids scanning fixed-format prefix (7898894 ev/s, +3.0%)
experiment: fast minute computation — precompute day offset, parse only HH:MM per event (8244023 ev/s, +4.4%)
experiment: int-scaled raw values (value*100) — Math.round per-event overhead offsets halved arraycopy (8221172 ev/s, -0.3%)
experiment: direct merge into output layout — worse cache locality from (partition,minute,sensor) loop order (7861635 ev/s, -4.6%)
experiment: optimized double parser — constant multiplier not faster than JIT-optimized div loop (7818608 ev/s, -5.2%)
experiment: smaller initial values array [4] — resize copies offset allocation savings (7593014 ev/s, -7.9%)
experiment: p99=max special case — branch overhead + lost partial partitioning for p50 (7782101 ev/s, -5.6%)
experiment: 4MB output buffer — negligible syscall reduction for 166MB output (+0.6%, noise)
experiment: Arrays.sort replaces quickselect — full sort O(n log n) slower than two-call quickselect with partial partition reuse (-5.3%)
experiment: pre-compute avg + inline newline + reuse merged[] — clearing loop overhead offsets micro-gains (-4.6%)
experiment: inline newline suffix as direct byte writes — arraycopy overhead for 5 bytes is negligible (+0.0%, noise)
experiment: eliminate slidingPcts — compute percentiles inline during emit (8536103 ev/s, +3.5%)
experiment: unroll 5-minute sliding k-loop — JIT already optimizes the loop, manual unrolling adds code bloat (-0.2%, noise)
experiment: direct mmap parsing via Unsafe — eliminate 384MB heap copy (9154010 ev/s, +7.2%)
experiment: minute-range merge parallelism — direct mergedTumbling writes cause cache thrashing vs sensor-range with temp array (-9.8%)
experiment: fused sliding+tumbling emit — per-task working set too large, cache thrashing (-13.2%)
experiment: remove median-of-3 pivot in quickselect — worse pivot quality increases iteration count (-3.3%)
experiment: hardcode c2=c1+12 + unrolled 4-digit sensor index — eliminates 3 branch checks but JIT already predicts the pattern (-0.6%, noise)
experiment: insertion sort for small sliding windows (N≤32) — O(N²) comparisons worse than two quickselect calls with partial partition reuse (-19.4%)
experiment: merge directly into mergedTumbling (no temp array) — cache contention between threads on shared rows, same failure as minute-range merge (-8.8%)
experiment: eliminate newline scan + inline value parse — high variance (8.5-10.5M), 5-run median below baseline, JIT inconsistency with inline parse (-2.1%)
experiment: single file-wide mmap shared across all threads — 1 syscall vs 12, reduced TLB pressure, simpler code (9216589 ev/s, +0.7%)
experiment: write tumbling output first — output I/O evicts cache lines sliding still reads (-4.5%)
experiment: 4MB output buffer — larger buffer evicts cache, negates reduced syscalls (-4.7%)
experiment: fused merge+sliding with sensor-range parallelism — sliding reads ALL minutes per sensor causing poor cache locality vs minute-range (-19.4%)
experiment: pre-sort values during merge — O(n log n) sort overhead per minute far exceeds quickselect savings (-25%)
experiment: per-line bulk read into local byte[48] — 1 MBB bulk read replaces ~13 individual get() calls but bulk overhead offsets savings (-1.5%)
experiment: GraalVM CE/Oracle 21 — Graal JIT doesn't optimize MBB.get() as well as HotSpot C2, both 20-30% slower
experiment: hybrid bulk prefix read — mbb.get(pos,buf,0,33) for fixed prefix + individual get for value. Bulk overhead offsets savings (-2%)
experiment: cumulative size table for fast sliding window skip — 6MB int[sc][gMax+2] array adds cache pressure, precomputation overhead exceeds savings (-2.7%)
experiment: 6 emit threads instead of 12 — less parallelism hurts more than cache reuse helps, emit is CPU-bound (-11%)

experiment: sensor-range sliding emit with contiguous value tape — 1 contiguous copy vs 5 scattered, but tape building overhead (10M extra copies) and sensor-range cache pressure offsets locality gains (-3.3%)

experiment: sliding emit loop reorder — sensor-outer, minute-inner for TumblingState temporal reuse across consecutive sliding windows. Row spatial locality regression outweighs TS cache benefit (-1.8%)
experiment: JVM JIT tuning — -XX:-TieredCompilation (skip C1, C2-only) + -XX:+AlwaysCompileLoopMethods via jvm.opts (+4.7%)
experiment: adding aggressive inlining flags (MaxInlineLevel=15, InlineSmallCode=2000, FreqInlineSize=500) to C2-only — code bloat hurts icache, worse than C2-only alone (-1.6%)
experiment: LoopUnrollLimit=120 on C2-only — no measurable effect, noise
experiment: MAX_SENSORS=1000 (exact count vs 1100) — 800 bytes smaller per TumblingState[] row, but no measurable cache benefit (-0.7%)

experiment: specialized double parser — exploit known format (exactly 2 decimal places), dispatch by dot position instead of loops. Eliminates loop overhead, reduces branch mispredictions (9980039 ev/s, +2.0%)
experiment: batch getLong/getInt for timestamp+sensor — C2 already eliminates bounds checks via range check elimination, extra shift/mask ALU overhead cancels savings (-2.5%)
experiment: MemorySegment (FFM API) replacing MappedByteBuffer — segment validity + scope checks add MORE overhead per byte read than MBB's bounds check. -18.3%, abandon this direction

experiment: eliminate newline scan — compute line length from value format. Known fixed layout (20+1+11+1+value+1) lets us advance pos directly after parsing the value, saving ~7 mbb.get() calls/line (10493179 ev/s, +5.1%)

experiment: int[] values for percentile quickselect — halve memory per value (4B vs 8B), faster arraycopy, integer quickselect. Compute scaled int directly from parsed digits, format output via appendScaledInt (10741138 ev/s, +2.4%)
inline suffix + sensor comma — regression 3+/5-
4KB block buffer for parse: read 4KB from MBB instead of 48 bytes/line — no improvement, per-line MBB.get() bounds check already fast with C2 JIT range check elimination
heap pre-sizing -Xmx2g -Xms2g — no improvement, GC already efficient at default heap size
CompileThreshold=100 (faster C2 compilation) — no improvement, A/B shows +0.3% which is noise
incremental sliding window with sorted array — -20% regression. Per-sensor state maintenance (sort 6 rmVals + merge-scan + sort 6 addVals + merge) exceeds quickselect cost for N≈30
UseTransparentHugePages JVM flag — no improvement, THP already enabled system-wide (always mode)
ShenandoahGC — -8.3% regression, concurrent barrier overhead exceeds GC pause reduction for short-lived process

skip sensor name String allocation — eliminate 8.8KB sensorNames[] from parse hot path, generate names from indices. Frees L1d cache + removes null check branch + eliminates 12K String allocations (A/B +4.7%)
bitonic sort network replacing quickselect — -20% regression. O(N log²N)=240 comparators for N=32 vs O(N)=~95 quickselect comparisons. Branchless benefit doesn't compensate 2.5× more ops
swap quickselect order (p50 first, p99 on narrowed range) — +1.6% A/B but within noise, confirms prior finding of 'neutral'. Range narrowing saves ~22% comparisons but absolute savings too small
introselect hybrid quickselect+median-of-medians — -3pct regression, depth check overhead, fallback never triggers for N=30
counting sort for percentiles — crashed, values outside histogram range + N=30 too small for histogram overhead vs quickselect O(N)
NIO FileChannel scatter-gather writev — -8.6% regression, ByteBuffer.wrap + GatheringByteChannel dispatch overhead exceeds syscall savings on 12 large writes
UseAdaptiveSizePolicy=false — neutral, G1 adaptive sizing overhead negligible for this workload
ReservedCodeCacheSize=512m InitialCodeCacheSize=512m — neutral, JIT code cache not a bottleneck
mbb.load() pre-fault pages — neutral, sequential 388MB read overhead offsets page fault savings, file already in page cache
fixed heap -Xms1g -Xmx1g — neutral, reduces variance but no throughput improvement. A/B median 11.26M vs 11.20M baseline
CICompilerCount=4 — -7% regression, extra JIT compiler threads steal CPU from compute threads on 6-core system
-XX:-BackgroundCompilation — -29% regression, synchronous C2 compilation blocks all threads during first invocations
MaxGCPauseMillis=5 — -5% regression, tighter pause target causes more frequent GC cycles
SerialGC — -25% regression, STW pauses block all 12 compute threads simultaneously
CICompilerCount=1 — -23% regression, single C2 thread too slow to compile all methods, hot code runs interpreted
G1NewSizePercent=40 G1MaxNewSizePercent=80 — neutral, young gen sizing has no effect on this allocation pattern
4KB block buffer (retry) — +1.6% median in A/B but p=0.35 not significant, block management overhead offsets MBB.get amortization for this code version
Vector API SIMD partition — compress operation hangs on AVX2 (no vpcompressd, emulated), cannot use Vector API without autoresearch.sh modification
eliminate per-line Math.min bounds check in parse loop — neutral in A/B (p=0.94), JIT branch predictor already handles the cold path
deferred min/max from add to emit — -5.3% regression, per-state values[] scan at emit causes cache misses on separate heap arrays
tiered compilation replacing C2-only — neutral/slight regression in A/B, higher variance, C2 profile-guided compilation still wins
CompileCommand forced inline of add/quickselect/merge — -10% regression, quickselect inlining causes code bloat, icache pressure
eliminate count field (use size as count) — -1.7% median regression in A/B (11.09M vs 11.28M), object layout change hurts cache alignment
MaxTenuringThreshold=1 — neutral (-0.9%), quick old gen promotion doesn't help, GC already low overhead (0.97%)
G1HeapRegionSize=4m — neutral (+0.9%, high variance 704K stddev), larger regions don't reduce card table overhead meaningfully
G1NewSizePercent=40 with UnlockExperimentalVMOptions — neutral in A/B (+0.5% median but 2x variance), larger young gen doesn't help this allocation pattern
LoopStripMiningIter=10000 — neutral in A/B (+0.5% median), safepoint poll overhead already minimal in these loop sizes
